audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Timothy text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for final charge, preach/proclaim, itching ears, poured out, good fight, abandonment, rescue, greetings.

verses.restored: I solemnly testify before the Divine and Messiah Jesus, the One about to judge living and dead, and by his appearing and his reign:
proclaim the word; stand ready in season and out of season; reprove, rebuke, encourage, with all patience and teaching.
For there will be a season when they will not endure healthy teaching, but according to their own desires they will heap up teachers for themselves, itching in hearing,
and indeed they will turn hearing away from the truth and will turn aside to myths.
But you be sober in all things, endure hardship, do work of one announcing good news, fulfill your service.
For I am already being poured out, and the season of my departure has stood near.
I have struggled the beautiful/good struggle; I have completed the course; I have kept the trust.

From now on the crown of righteousness/justice is stored up for me, which the Lord, the righteous/just judge, will repay to me in that day, and not only to me but also to all those having loved his appearing.
Be eager to come to me quickly,
for Demas, having loved the present age, abandoned me and went to Thessalonica; Crescens to Galatia, Titus to Dalmatia.
Luke alone is with me. Taking Mark, bring him with you, for he is useful to me for service.
But Tychicus I sent to Ephesus.
The cloak that I left in Troas with Carpus, bring when you come, and the scrolls, especially the parchments.
Alexander the metalworker did many harmful things to me; the Lord will repay him according to his works.
You also guard yourself from him, for he greatly opposed our words.
At my first defense no one came alongside me, but all abandoned me; may it not be counted against them.
But the Lord stood by me and empowered me, so that through me the proclamation might be fulfilled and all the nations might hear; and I was rescued from lion's mouth.
The Lord will rescue me from every harmful work and save/heal me into his heavenly reign; to him be glory into the ages of the ages. Amen.
Greet Prisca and Aquila and the household of Onesiphorus.
Erastus remained in Corinth, but Trophimus I left sick in Miletus.
Be eager to come before winter. Eubulus greets you, and Pudens and Linus and Claudia and all the siblings.
The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you.

translation_notes: Final charge is proclamation with patience and teaching, not domination.
The crown of righteousness/justice is linked to faithful endurance and longing for appearing.
Paul's prayer that abandonment not be counted against them preserves mercy even in loneliness.
